analyses.Analyses: .TRAN, with LTE-controlled adaptive timestepping and checkpoint/resume segmentation
Operating-point Monte Carlo parameter variation, process corners
Periodic steady state (shooting), harmonic balance, two-port S-parameters with Touchstone export
.MEAS over TRAN/DC/AC/NOISE with GOAL/TOL pass-fail gating, .FOUR; THD/IMD, eye-diagram, and jitter metrics

benchmarks.methodology: wall-clock of the full child process (parse + solve + output) timed with std::time::Instant around spawn/wait; child stdin/stdout/stderr attached to the null device; one untimed warmup run per deck/simulator precedes the timed repeats; cold OS cache not controlled

cli_tables.context: RSpice CLI / Commands / rspice run — Execute Simulations / Output options
rows: Output file for results. With several analysis cards in one deck, each analysis writes its own tagged file: out.csv → out.op.csv, out.tran.csv, ...
Output format: raw, ascii, csv, json, tsv, hdf5 (default: config output.format, else raw)
Limit exported signals, replacing the netlist .SAVE/.PROBE selection: V(out), V(a,b), I(v1), @m1[id], all (repeatable)
Write a JSON run summary — tool version, per-run status, every measurement, the result files written, overall verdict — to FILE, or stdout with -
Show a live percentage bar during transient analysis (the engine reports its completed fraction)
Enable waveform compression for long simulations
Compression tolerance (default: config compressiontolerance, else 1e-4; requires --compress)

cli_tables.context: RSpice CLI / Commands / rspice run — Execute Simulations / Run-discipline options
rows: Abort the run after this budget; exits 124. Transient and DC sweeps stop at the next safe point. Ctrl-C stops the same way and exits 130
Exit 0 even when .MEAS measurements fail (default: exit 3)
Export results containing NaN/Inf instead of failing (default: simulation error)
Save the transient integrator state when the run completes, for later --resume
Continue a transient from a saved checkpoint; the deck must be byte-identical (fingerprint-checked) and a segmented run reproduces the uninterrupted waveform
Override the .TRAN stop time without editing the deck, so checkpoint segments share identical source

cli_tables.context: RSpice CLI / Commands / rspice run — Execute Simulations / Simulation options
rows: Relative residual tolerance for equation convergence checks
Transient integrator: trap, gear, trapgear (default), euler
Initial GMIN conductance for convergence aids
DC convergence preset: fast, default, robust
Add a search directory for .include/.lib references (repeatable)
Override or define a netlist parameter; values accept SPICE suffixes, e.g. -D RLOAD=4.7k (repeatable)

cli_tables.context: RSpice CLI / Commands / rspice run — Execute Simulations / Analysis-mode options
rows: Run N Monte Carlo iterations (operating-point variation); independent runs solve in parallel across cores with seed-stable sampling
Random seed for Monte Carlo; the default seed 1 makes runs reproducible (requires --monte-carlo)
Relative spread: sigma for gaussian, tolerance otherwise (default: 0.01)
PSS stabilization time before the shooting method (default: auto)
Harmonic balance fundamental frequency in Hz
Sensitivity analysis output node (name or index)

Two-port S-parameter extraction over the deck's .AC sweep: four port nodes as "P1+,P1-,P2+,P2-" (use 0 for grounded references). Writes Touchstone when -o ends in .s2p, else the standard complex table formats
Library with one .lib <corner> ... .endl section per corner; each corner re-elaborates the deck with its section applied (requires --corners)
Parallel workers for corner sweeps (default: 1). Corner outputs are tagged per corner so workers never collide; results are byte-identical to a serial sweep

cli_tables.context: RSpice CLI / Commands / rspice run — Execute Simulations / Reporting options
rows: .MEAS output file (defaults to JSON if --meas-format is omitted)

cli_tables.context: RSpice CLI / Commands / rspice info — Netlist Information
RSpice CLI / Commands / rspice check — Validate Netlist
RSpice CLI / Commands / rspice compare — Golden File Comparison
rows: Compare specific variables only (repeatable; default: all)
Tolerate point-count mismatches and compare the overlap
Tolerate golden variables missing from the result
Accept the result as the new reference: copies it over the golden file when they differ, or creates the golden file if it does not exist
Linearly resample the result onto the golden file's scale, so runs with different time grids compare point-for-point (never extrapolates)

cli_tables.context: RSpice CLI / Commands / rspice convert — Format Conversion
rows: Input format (auto-detected from the extension if omitted)
Variables to keep, by full (V(out)) or bare (out) name (repeatable; default: all)
Keep points with scale ≥ this time/frequency
Keep points with scale ≤ this time/frequency

cli_tables.context: RSpice CLI / Commands / rspice compile-va — Compile Verilog-A
rows: Write a JSON interface summary: model name, terminals, internal node count, parameters with defaults and bounds
Generate a usage example in the output
Include search paths, platform path separator (; on Windows, : elsewhere)
Model library paths, platform path separator
Success: simulation ran and every check passed
Simulation error (convergence failure, non-finite results, Verilog-A compile failure, conversion failure)
Verification failure: a .MEAS failed or did not evaluate, or compare found mismatches
Input format error (netlist parse failure, singular topology from check)
I/O error (failed to read input or write output)

commands.Description: Compare output against a golden result
Convert between RAW, ASCII RAW, CSV, JSON, TSV, and HDF5

devices.Models: Native BSIM4 v4.8 (LEVEL=14/54, canonical mode set), BSIM3v3.3 (LEVEL=8/9/49, CAPMOD=2/3), VDMOS, EKV3, Berkeley MOS1/MOS2/MOS3/MOS6, and legacy BSIM1/BSIM2; generated Verilog-A builtins are canonical for BSIM-SOI (LEVEL=10/55/56/57) and EKV 2.6 (LEVEL=260); unsupported MOS levels fail closed until generated or native support and validation are added

Gummel-Poon BJT (default / LEVEL=1) plus generated Verilog-A builtins for VBIC (LEVEL=4/9/11/12/13), HICUM, and MEXTRAM where bundled sources are available; unsupported advanced BJT levels fail explicitly until generated support lands
Diode, JFET level 1 and native Parker-Skellern JFET2 (NJF/PJF LEVEL=2, default/best-available), an internal Xyce modified-Shockley JFET2 compatibility mode, MES/MESA/HFET-family Z devices, GaN HEMT
R / C / L with temperature coefficients, coupled inductors and multi-winding transformers, saturable inductor (Jiles–Atherton hysteresis)
Independent V/I with PULSE, SIN, EXP, PWL, SFFM, AM, and TRNOISE white + 1/f waveforms; E/F/G/H controlled sources; B behavioral sources; PWL file sources
Voltage- and current-controlled switches, op-amp macromodel
XSPICE-style analog/digital elements, tri-state drivers, A/D–D/A bridges

workspace.Purpose: Simulation engine: device models, analyses, netlist parser, validation harnesses
Command-line interface for simulation, validation, conversion, and reporting
Desktop application for schematic editing and waveform inspection
Verilog-A parser, semantic pipeline, bytecode VM/native JIT runtime, generated-Rust backend
WebAssembly bindings for the simulation engine
Whole-process benchmark rig against local ngspice
